questions.questionText: Which construction creates the perpendicular bisector of a line segment AB?
options: Drawing arcs from A and B with the same radius (greater than half AB), intersecting at two points, then drawing a line through these points.
Measuring the midpoint of AB and drawing a line perpendicular to AB through it.
Drawing a line segment from A to B and then bisecting angle A.
Drawing a circle with diameter AB.
questions.explanation: The perpendicular bisector is constructed by drawing arcs from each end of the line segment with a radius greater than half the segment's length. The line connecting the intersection points of these arcs is the perpendicular bisector.

questions.questionText: To bisect an angle, the first step is to place the compass point on the vertex and draw an arc that intersects which parts of the angle?
options: The two arms of the angle.
The line segment connecting the two arms.
Any point outside the angle.
The midpoint of one arm.
questions.explanation: The first step in bisecting an angle is to draw an arc from the vertex that intersects both arms of the angle.

questions.questionText: Which geometric shape is fundamental to constructing a 60° angle using a compass and straightedge?
options: A square
An equilateral triangle
A right-angled triangle
An isosceles triangle
questions.explanation: An equilateral triangle has all angles equal to 60°. Constructing one side, then arcs from its ends to locate the third vertex, naturally creates a 60° angle.

questions.questionText: You want to construct a line perpendicular to line L at a point P on line L. What is the initial step using a compass?
options: Draw an arc from P intersecting L at two points equidistant from P.
Draw an arc from a point not on L, passing through P.
Draw a line from P to any point above L.
Place the compass at an arbitrary point on L.
questions.explanation: To construct a perpendicular line at a point P on line L, first draw an arc from P that intersects line L at two points equidistant from P. These two points then serve as centers for further arcs to find a point off the line.

questions.questionText: To construct a triangle with sides 5 cm, 7 cm, and 8 cm, using a compass and straightedge, which length would you typically draw first as the base?
options: 5 cm
7 cm
8 cm
Any length, it doesn't matter.
questions.explanation: To construct a triangle given three side lengths (SSS), first draw one of the sides as a base. While any side can be chosen, drawing the longest side (8 cm) first can often make the subsequent arc intersections easier to manage within the drawing space.

questions.questionText: A construction starts with an angle. An arc is drawn from the vertex, intersecting both arms. From these intersection points, two new arcs are drawn with the same radius, intersecting each other. A line is then drawn from the vertex through this intersection. What has been constructed?
options: A perpendicular bisector
A parallel line
An angle bisector
A perpendicular line
questions.explanation: This describes the standard procedure for constructing an angle bisector.

questions.questionText: A line segment AB is drawn. With the compass point at A, an arc is drawn above and below AB. With the same compass setting, an arc is drawn from B, intersecting the previous arcs. A line is drawn through these intersection points. What has been constructed?
options: An angle bisector of angle A
The perpendicular bisector of line segment AB
A line parallel to AB
A 60° angle at A
questions.explanation: This is the method for constructing the perpendicular bisector of a line segment.

questions.questionText: The locus of points equidistant from two fixed points A and B is what type of line?
options: A line parallel to AB
The angle bisector of an angle formed by A and B
The perpendicular bisector of the line segment AB
A line passing through A and B
questions.explanation: The perpendicular bisector of a line segment AB contains all points that are equidistant from A and B.

questions.questionText: The locus of points equidistant from two intersecting lines is what type of line?
options: A line parallel to one of the intersecting lines
The perpendicular bisector of the line segment connecting the intersection points
The angle bisector of the angle formed by the intersecting lines
A circle centered at the intersection point
questions.explanation: The angle bisector of an angle formed by two intersecting lines contains all points that are equidistant from those two lines.

questions.questionText: To construct a 90° angle at a point on a straight line using a compass and straightedge, which construction method is most directly applicable?
options: Bisecting an angle of 180°.
Constructing an equilateral triangle.
Constructing the perpendicular bisector of a line segment not on the line.
Bisecting a 60° angle.
questions.explanation: A straight line forms a 180° angle. Bisecting this straight angle at a point on the line will create two 90° angles, which is a perpendicular line.

questions.questionText: When constructing a perpendicular bisector of line segment PQ, a student draws arcs from P and Q. What is a crucial error if the arcs do not intersect?
options: The compass radius used was too small.
The compass radius used was too large.
The arcs were drawn from the wrong endpoints.
The straightedge was not used correctly.
questions.explanation: For arcs to intersect when constructing a perpendicular bisector, the compass radius used from each endpoint must be greater than half the length of the line segment. If they don't intersect, the radius was too small.

questions.questionText: When performing geometric constructions, why is it important to leave all construction lines visible?
options: To show working and allow verification of the construction.
To make the diagram look more complex.
To save time by not erasing them.
Because they are part of the final answer.
questions.explanation: Leaving construction lines visible demonstrates the method used and allows the accuracy of the construction to be checked, serving as evidence of the process.
